Identity.History: Cataphracts were heavily armored shock cavalry used by several ancient empires. The Seleucids adopted them from Iranian and Central Asian traditions, fielding armored horsemen with long lances to break enemy lines. The Persians, especially the Parthians and Sasanians, made cataphracts central to elite cavalry warfare, combining them with horse archers. The Romans first faced cataphracts as enemies, then gradually adopted similar units, especially in the eastern empire. Though expensive and demanding to maintain, cataphracts offered devastating charge power and became a symbol of aristocratic military prestige.
